adventures.title: The Traitor's Gambit
adventures.summary: The heroes are drawn into a bar brawl on Jakku, discover a stolen Imperial code cylinder, rescue a frozen accountant, and break Admiral Varth out of a jungle prison — all for the promise of a stolen fortune.

gearNotes.reason: Toxic fungal spores in the Ajan Kloss jungle. Without respiratory protection, characters take environmental penalties on all physical actions during the trek.
Jungle traversal — crossing gaps, climbing, rappelling. Without rope, certain routes become impossible or require much harder Athletics checks. The item everyone forgets to buy.
The Vanishing Place fortress has electronic locks, laser fences, and security panels. Without one, bypassing these is stepped down or impossible.
Generator sabotage as part of Mandrake's deal. Without proper tools, Tech actions are stepped down.
The Banshee crashes in the jungle. Without a starship tool kit, all repair rolls are stepped down. Hard permission gate for getting off-world.
Multiple combats across both parts with no medical facilities. At least one is non-negotiable.
The Niima Landing Field read-aloud literally references macrobinoculars for recon. Also useful for jungle scouting.
Acid pools in the jungle are disguised as standing water. A tox detector identifies them without someone stepping in one.
Dim cantina, underground bunker, dense jungle canopy — multiple dark environments across both parts.
The bluff approach at the Niima hangar requires impersonating Imperial crew or authorized personnel.
Covering retreat across open ground during the three-way climax with Shadow Troopers flanking.
Mid-combat trauma clearance. Valuable in the multi-fight climax sequence.
Extended jungle trek. Fatigue penalties stack after 48 hours without food.
Jakku desert heat in Part 1. Environmental comfort only — no hard gate.
Coordinating with Okeefe for extraction timing. Encrypted channel prevents Imperial interception.
Hacking terminals in the fortress Comms Center to intercept transmissions about Draco's arrival.

scenes.title: Bad Business at Sienar's Regret
scenes.subtitle: In Media Res
scenes.readAloud:
Jakku. A world the galaxy forgot — if it ever noticed. Out here on the Western Reaches, the holonet feeds are weeks old, the Republic is a memory, and the Empire is a rumor that sometimes wears a uniform. You've heard the stories filtering through the spacer lanes: whole systems pacified, Separatist holdouts burned out of their bunkers, the clone army quietly replaced by men who volunteer. The Core worlds call it order. Out here, people just call it the new way things are.

Niima Outpost squats on the desert flats like something the sand is trying to swallow. Scavengers, smugglers, moisture farmers with nothing left to farm. Darga the Hutt runs what passes for an economy — debt, protection, and the quiet understanding that Imperial patrols from Arkanis Sector come through maybe twice a standard year, and they never stay.

Except today. You noticed it when you docked: a Lambda-class shuttle sitting on the landing field, flanked by two stormtroopers standing in the heat like they were carved from plastoid. Nobody docks a Lambda on Jakku without a reason. The streets were quieter on the walk in. Fewer faces in the stalls. The regulars at Sienar's Regret are nursing their drinks a little harder, talking a little softer. Something has changed the arithmetic, and nobody knows the new numbers yet.

You've been here an hour. Long enough for the ice in your drinks to surrender to the heat. Long enough to notice the bartender checking the door every few minutes.

Then the blast doors hiss open.

A young human woman stumbles through — clutching her side where a blaster burn has scorched through her jacket. Blood seeps between her fingers, but she's moving fast. She scans the room with desperate eyes, spots your table, and crashes into it, sending drinks and credits scattering across the floor.

"They're coming," she gasps, pressing a datapad against the table. "Please — just look like you're with me."

Before you can respond, her pursuers fill the doorway: a heavy-set Weequay and two Kyuzo enforcers wearing wide-brimmed war helms. Their blasters are holstered. They're not here to kill — they're here to collect. The Weequay rolls his shoulders, looks at your table, and makes a decision. "The girl owes Darga the Hutt. You want to be part of her debt?"
scenes.gmNotes:
This is the campaign's inciting incident — but it is NOT an assassination. The thugs are debt collectors for Darga the Hutt's local operation. They have NOT drawn weapons. Maya deliberately crashed into the heroes' table to create the impression they're her crew — she used them as a shield.

NARRATIVE PACING: This scene runs on narrative pacing. Do NOT call for dice unless failure forks the story. Maya crashing in — narrative. Krev's ultimatum — narrative. The bartender's nervousness — narrative. Only roll when a PC takes an action where failure has real consequences (escalation, loss of initiative, or missed intelligence).

THE IMPERIAL SHUTTLE: Foreshadowing only. The Lambda on the landing field is not part of this scene — it seeds future tension. If players investigate, the stormtroopers say nothing. Locals don't know why it's here. This pays off later.

MAYA'S WOUND: Real. A blaster graze to her side from a warning shot outside the cantina. Non-lethal but painful and bleeding. She is desperate, not performing — but she IS calculating. She chose this table because she saw armed people sitting at it.

RESOLUTION PATHS:
• QUICK-DRAW INTIMIDATION: A Gunslinger draws fast — blaster out before the thugs can react. This IS the Intimidate check (Moderate), not a separate action. The draw is the statement. The blaster is leverage, not escalation. Success: Krev folds ('Not worth it today'). Failure: Krev calls the bluff ('You won't shoot over some girl you don't know') — now the PC must decide: pull the trigger (firefight) or holster (Krev smells weakness, uses Lean In).
• SOCIAL: Charm, Deception, or Persuasion (Moderate) can convince the thugs Maya isn't worth the trouble, redirect them, or negotiate a delay.
• BRAWL: If anyone throws a punch, the Kyuzo respond in kind. Bar brawl — fists, headbutts, bottles. Stun damage. Blasters stay holstered.
• FIREFIGHT: Only triggers if (a) a brawl is already happening AND someone draws a blaster, OR (b) the social check fails AND a PC with blaster already drawn refuses to hand Maya over. A drawn blaster alone is not an escalation — it's a negotiating tool. This is Star Wars.

IF COMBAT TRIGGERS (FIRST COMBAT TUTORIAL): This may be the players' first tactical encounter. Walk them through it:
• Initiative: NPCs use static initiative (Wits + Tier). PCs roll normally.
• Zones: The cantina is a 4×3 grid of 15-foot zones (see tacticalMap). Moving one zone = one maneuver. Two zones = your whole turn.
• Cover: Explain Find Cover maneuver ('You can flip that table — that's a maneuver, gives you light cover'). Point out the bar as hard cover.
• Maneuvers: Each turn you get one maneuver and one action. Maneuvers are movement, drawing weapons, flipping tables, grabbing bottles. Actions are attacks, social checks, using abilities.
• Krev uses Loom (maneuver) to advance and Threaten (action) for social pressure. If brawl, he switches to Physique attacks.
• Kyuzo use Reposition (maneuver, no reactions provoked) to flank — one to A2, one to D2. Then Hit and Run (action) for war-helm headbutts.

AFTER RESOLUTION: Maya offers a business proposition, not a noble speech. She has stolen an encrypted Imperial Code Cylinder from Darga the Hutt's operation and claims it leads to a stash of embezzled credits. She needs the heroes to help decrypt it in exchange for a cut.

KEY LORE: 16 BBY. Imperial presence on Jakku is thin — Arkanis Sector patrols, no garrison. The Lambda shuttle today is unusual and unsettling. Darga the Hutt runs the real economy here: smuggling, debt, protection. A cantina fight draws local attention (Darga's people, scavenger gangs) but NOT Imperial attention — the troopers at the landing field don't care about Hutt business.

npcs.name: Maya
npcs.notes: Desperate but calculating. She used the heroes as muscle.
npcs.behavior: Stays behind the heroes, clutching her wound. If combat starts, she ducks behind the bar. After resolution, switches to confident pitch mode.
dialogue: "They're coming. Please — just look like you're with me."
"I have something worth more than what I owe. A lot more. But I need people with blasters to collect it."
"The wound? A disagreement about payment terms. They're not subtle."
npcs.intel: Maya stole an encrypted Imperial Code Cylinder from Darga's operation. She doesn't know exactly what's on it — she thought it was vault coordinates.

npcs.name: Krev Tosk
npcs.notes: Darga's local muscle. Senior collector. A professional — he collects debts, not corpses.
npcs.behavior: Opens with Loom (maneuver): fills the doorway, rolls his shoulders, walks to the PCs' table. Then Threaten (action): social pressure using implied violence. Will back down from a genuine show of force — a fast-drawn blaster makes him recalculate. Does NOT draw his own weapon unless the social situation has completely collapsed AND violence is already happening. If the Intimidate check fails, uses Lean In to escalate pressure. In a brawl, fights with fists (Physique 3) — grapples, shoves, uses furniture.
dialogue: "The girl owes Darga the Hutt. You want to be part of her debt?"
"Walk away. This isn't your business yet."
"Darga remembers faces. Think about that."
"You won't shoot over some girl you don't know."
npcs.intel: Knows Maya stole something from Darga's warehouse. Doesn't know what.

passive.name: Implied Violence
passive.description: Social attacks can inflict [Shaken] instead of normal social conditions.

actions.name: Threaten
actions.description: Social attack using implied force. Targets Resist.

actions.name: Lean In
actions.description: Escalate social pressure. +1 Power if target already has a social condition.

maneuver.name: Loom
maneuver.description: Move to close range. Target must resist or gain [Rattled].

gambit.name: Break Something
gambit.description: Demonstrate capacity for violence. All witnesses gain [Shaken].

exploit.name: Last Warning
exploit.description: If target has [Shaken], automatically escalate to [Incapacitated] (surrender/flee).

threatBuild.notes: Power 4 = (effective Physique 4 - 1) + Tier 1. Defense 4 = max(4,2)-1+1. Vitality 7 = Phys 4 + Grit 2 + Tier 1. Wits clamped to 1 (base 1, Enforcer -1, floor 1). Vs a starting PC with Resist ~3, net +1 = Fleeting social hit. Enough to apply pressure, not devastating.

npcs.name: Kyuzo Enforcers
npcs.notes: Wide-brimmed war helms. Armed but holstered. Follow Krev's lead. Silent and disciplined.
npcs.behavior: During social phase: silent and watchful, use Reposition (maneuver) to cut off exits — one moves to booth row (A2), one to alcove (D2). Do not initiate violence. In a brawl: war-helm headbutts (Hit and Run — melee attack + free disengage). Fight as a pair to flank. As Minions, Masterful+ result = instant takedown. They are speed bumps, not real threats.

passive.name: Fleet Footed
passive.description: May take 2 maneuvers per turn instead of 1.

actions.name: Harrying Shot
actions.description: Ranged attack at close range. Target cannot take reactions until next turn.

exploit.name: Ambush
exploit.description: If attacking from stealth or flanking, +2 Power on the attack.

threatBuild.notes: Power 3 = (effective Reflex 4 - 1) + Tier 0. Defense 3 = max(2,4)-1+0. Evasion 3 = Ref 4 - 1 + 0. Resist 0 = Grit 1 - 1 + 0 (extremely vulnerable to social/grit attacks). Vitality 3 = Phys 2 + Grit 1 + Tier 0 (fragile). Minion: Masterful+ = instant takedown. They exist to make the room feel dangerous, not to be a real fight.

scenes.hazards: Cantina environment — dim lighting, overturned tables, broken glass, panicking patrons. A fight on Jakku draws local attention (Darga's people, scavenger gangs) but NOT Imperial attention.

decisions.consequence: The heroes are now entangled in a heist. Maya offers the Code Cylinder job.

decisions.choice: Try to stay out of it
decisions.consequence: The thugs assume the heroes are Maya's muscle regardless — combat is unavoidable.

narrativeLinks.note: Darga's enforcers here foreshadow the full encounter with his organization on Takodana.

encounters.name: The Collection
encounters.trigger: Immediate — thugs in the doorway
encounters.description: Darga's collectors confront Maya at the heroes' table. Debt collection, not a hit. Heroes must decide whether to get involved. This is narrative pacing — no dice until a PC takes an action where failure matters.
encounters.tactics: Krev uses Loom (maneuver) — walks to the table, fills the space. Then Threaten (action) — social pressure via implied violence. Kyuzo silently Reposition to flank (one toward A2 booths, one toward D2 alcove). If a hero draws a blaster, that's leverage — it feeds into the Intimidate check, not a trigger for combat. Krev reassesses: is this debt worth a blaster bolt? If the Intimidate succeeds, he folds. If it fails, he calls the bluff and uses Lean In.

encounters.name: Cantina Brawl
encounters.trigger: Only if someone throws the first punch, or social resolution fails badly and PCs refuse to back down
encounters.description: Bar brawl in a cramped cantina. Fists, headbutts, thrown bottles, overturned tables. Stun damage — no one is trying to kill. Blasters stay holstered. Combat only escalates to lethal if: (a) a brawl is already happening AND someone draws a blaster, OR (b) social failed AND a PC with blaster drawn refuses to hand Maya over.
encounters.tactics: Krev grapples and shoves (Physique 3). Kyuzo headbutt with war helms — Hit and Run (melee attack + free disengage), then Reposition to flank. They fight as a pair. If a blaster comes out during the brawl, all three draw — the scene shifts to lethal. Tutorial note: walk players through maneuvers (flip table = Find Cover, grab bottle = Equip improvised weapon, move one zone = maneuver).

composition.positioning: Krev at B1/C1 entrance, Kyuzo split B1 and C1. Kyuzo Reposition to A2 and D2 to flank.

encounters.name: Maya's Proposition
encounters.trigger: After the thugs are dealt with (any resolution path)
encounters.description: Maya shifts to a calculated pitch. The desperation is gone — replaced by a confidence that was always there underneath. She offers the heroes a cut of whatever the Code Cylinder leads to.
encounters.tactics: Maya reads the room — appeals to greed for mercenaries, justice for idealists, curiosity for investigators. This is narrative — no roll needed unless PCs actively resist the hook (and even then, the Code Cylinder is too interesting to walk away from).

skillChecks.context: Reading Maya — genuine desperation or performance? Only roll if a PC specifically asks. Failure matters: it hides that she chose this table on purpose, which colors trust going forward.
skillChecks.success: The wound is real. The fear is real. But she chose this table on purpose — she saw armed people and made a calculation.
skillChecks.failure: She seems genuinely terrified. No indication of ulterior motives.
skillChecks.narrativePacing: Roll only if asked. Do not prompt for this check.

skillChecks.context: The Gunslinger's fast draw IS this check — blaster out before the thugs can react. The draw is the statement, the blaster is leverage. Other vocations can use presence, posture, or reputation. This is the ONE roll that determines whether violence happens.
skillChecks.success: Krev reads the situation: this person is faster than his crew. 'Not worth it today.' Backs off, warns Darga remembers faces, leaves with the Kyuzo.
skillChecks.failure: Krev doesn't flinch. 'You won't shoot over some girl you don't know.' The PC now has a choice: pull the trigger (firefight) or holster it (Krev uses Lean In, smells weakness, situation gets worse).
skillChecks.narrativePacing: This is a story fork. Always roll.

skillChecks.context: Talking the thugs down or redirecting them. Failure matters: escalation.
skillChecks.success: Thugs are mollified or confused enough to withdraw.
skillChecks.failure: Krev sees through it. 'Nice try. Hand her over.'

skillChecks.context: Claiming Maya is under protection or the debt is settled. Failure matters: escalation.
skillChecks.success: Krev hesitates — leaves to confirm with his boss.
skillChecks.failure: 'I know everyone Darga deals with. You're nobody.'

skillChecks.context: Treating Maya's blaster wound after resolution.
skillChecks.success: Wound cleaned and dressed. Maya is grateful and more forthcoming.
skillChecks.failure: Basic patch job. Maya stays functional.
skillChecks.narrativePacing: Failure doesn't fork the story. Skip the roll unless a player specifically wants to treat her — reward the effort narratively.

environmentMechanics.name: Dim Cantina Lighting
environmentMechanics.effect: Failing glow-rods create shadow pools. Ranged attacks at distance stepped down.
environmentMechanics.mitigation: Close range unaffected. Rodian infrared / Sullustan darkvision ignore penalty.

environmentMechanics.name: Cantina Furniture
environmentMechanics.effect: Overturned tables = light cover. Bar = hard cover. Chairs and bottles as improvised weapons.
environmentMechanics.mitigation: Athletics check to flip a table for instant cover.

environmentMechanics.name: Panicking Patrons
environmentMechanics.effect: Civilians scramble for exits. Ranged attacks into crowds risk bystanders.
environmentMechanics.mitigation: Careful shot placement or waiting for clear lines.

zones.label: Solid Wall
zones.terrain: Duracrete wall. Impassable. Channels all traffic through B1/C1 entrance.

zones.label: Entrance (West)
zones.terrain: Blast door entrance. Jakku sunlight pours in. No cover — anyone standing here is fully exposed. Door controls on the wall.

zones.label: Entrance (East)
zones.terrain: Blast door entrance. Wide enough for three abreast. No cover. The glare from outside imposes no penalty but is narratively blinding.

zones.label: Solid Wall
zones.terrain: Duracrete wall. Impassable. Storage crates stacked against the outside.

zones.label: Booth Row
zones.terrain: Curved booth seating along the wall. Light cover from booth backs. Shadow pool — dim lighting penalty applies to ranged attacks targeting or from this zone at range. A few patrons nursing drinks.

zones.label: Center Floor (PCs' Table)
zones.terrain: The heroes' table. Light cover from furniture. A PC can use Find Cover (maneuver) to flip the table for improved light cover. Central position — adjacent to all Row 2 and Row 1/3 zones. This is where the action starts.

zones.label: Open Floor
zones.terrain: Open cantina floor. No cover, no obstacles. Clear sightlines to every other zone. The kill zone if blasters come out.

zones.label: Alcove
zones.terrain: Recessed alcove with a sabacc table. Light cover from the table edge. Deep shadow — dim lighting penalty applies. A patron or two hiding here.

zones.label: Back Hallway
zones.terrain: Narrow corridor to the back exit. No cover. Leads to the alley behind the cantina — the escape route. Dim lighting from a single failing glow-rod.

zones.label: Bar (West)
zones.terrain: West end of the bar counter. Hard cover — the bar is solid durasteel and wood. Bottles and glasses as improvised weapons (Light chassis, stun). The bartender's position. Maya flees here if combat starts.

zones.label: Bar (East)
zones.terrain: East end of the bar counter. Hard cover. Tap system and storage underneath. Same improvised weapon access as B3. Good defensive position with sightlines to the entrance.

zones.label: Store Room
zones.terrain: Closed door (requires maneuver to open). Full cover once inside. Dark — no light source. Crates of supplies, kegs. Dead end — defensible but no exit. A cornered position.

startingPositions.notes: Seated at their table. Central position with light cover.
Crashes into B2. Flees to B3 (bar, hard cover) if combat starts.
Enters through blast doors. Exposed — no cover. Advances to B2/C2 via Loom maneuver.
Behind Krev at entrance. Repositions to A2 (booth row) to flank.
Flanking the entrance. Repositions to D2 (alcove) to cut off the far side.
Scattered. Flee to B1/C1 (entrance) or A3 (back exit) if weapons are drawn.

tacticalMap.gmTacticalNotes: Krev starts exposed at B1 — intentional. He's a collector, not a tactician. He expects compliance. The Kyuzo use Fleet Footed (2 maneuvers/turn) + Reposition (no reactions) to silently flank to A2 and D2 during the social phase — cutting off the booth row and alcove. This is positioning, not aggression. If combat triggers: Krev advances to B2 or C2 for melee range. Kyuzo attack with Hit and Run from flanking positions — headbutt then disengage. The bar (B3/C3) is the strongest defensive position. The open floor (C2) is the worst — no cover, clear sightlines. The back hallway (A3) is the escape route. The store room (D3) is a trap — full cover but no exit. Teach players: 'You can flip that table (Find Cover maneuver). You can grab a bottle (Equip maneuver). Moving to the next zone costs your maneuver. Two zones costs your whole turn.'

intel: Maya stole from Darga the Hutt's operation
Cylinder needs a specialist — Switch in the Starship Graveyard

pacing.openingBeat: Narrative intro establishes Jakku, the galaxy at 16 BBY, the Imperial shuttle foreshadow, and the subdued cantina mood. Players settle into the setting before the action hits.
pacing.risingAction: Maya crashes in wounded. Thugs fill the doorway. Krev delivers his ultimatum. Kyuzo silently flank. Social pressure mounts.
pacing.climax: The decision — fast-draw intimidation, social play, or let it go to fists. One roll that matters.
pacing.resolution: Maya's pitch. Code Cylinder revealed. Hook set.

scenes.title: The Processor
scenes.readAloud:
Maya leads you out of the outpost and into the Starship Graveyard. You navigate through the ribs of fallen capital ships and dunes of rust until you reach a subterranean bunker built from buried shipping containers.

Inside is a technological cave — wires hang from the ceiling like vines, the floor is a carpet of droid parts. But in the center of the filth sits a pristine, priceless desk of Japor ivory wood. Behind it, a protocol droid who has clearly forgone memory wipes for decades regards you with an unsettling intelligence.
scenes.gmNotes: Switch is a crime lord and information broker, not an ally — he is a businessman holding all the cards. He agrees to decrypt the cylinder for a cut. The decryption reveals a twist: the cylinder doesn't contain vault coordinates. It contains a shipping manifest for a 'biological asset' (The Accountant) currently held in a local warlord's hangar. Switch should feel dangerous and calculating despite his droid body.

npcs.name: Switch
npcs.notes: Decades without memory wipes. Japor ivory desk. Businessman, not ally.
npcs.behavior: Calculated precision. Uses silence as a weapon. Always running cost-benefit analysis.
dialogue: "I don't do favors. I do transactions."
"The cylinder's encryption is Imperial-grade. Military. Decrypting it will cost you — not in credits. In obligation."
"The contents are... not what your friend expected. Shall I continue?"
npcs.intel: Knows cylinder was stolen from Darga. Knows Darga is connected to Imperial logistics. Suspects it's more valuable than Maya realizes.

scenes.hazards: Cramped underground bunker. Motion sensors active.

decisions.choice: Accept Switch's terms for the decryption
decisions.consequence: The cylinder is decrypted, revealing The Accountant's location.
Switch has security turrets and backup. Extremely dangerous.

narrativeLinks.note: Switch's agent TC-663 appears in Darga's court — connected through Switch's intelligence network.

encounters.name: The Deal with Switch
encounters.trigger: When heroes present the Code Cylinder
encounters.description: Switch names his price: a future favor, no questions asked. He won't accept credits — he wants leverage.
encounters.tactics: Patient and immovable. Heroes need him more than he needs them. Uses their desperation and Maya's impatience.

encounters.name: The Decryption Reveal
encounters.trigger: After the deal is struck
encounters.description: The cylinder doesn't contain vault coordinates. It's a shipping manifest for 'The Accountant' — an Imperial officer frozen in carbonite at Niima Landing Field.
encounters.tactics: Switch delivers with clinical detachment. Watches reactions carefully.

skillChecks.context: Negotiating Switch's price down from open-ended favor.
skillChecks.success: Switch agrees to a single, defined favor instead.
skillChecks.failure: Open-ended favor stands.

skillChecks.context: Reading Switch's motives.
skillChecks.success: Switch is building a network of obligations — positioning as the indispensable middleman.
skillChecks.failure: Switch is inscrutable.

skillChecks.context: Examining the decrypted data for hidden details.
skillChecks.success: Additional details: the asset was shipped from Eriadu. Shipping codes match 'Project Leviathan.'
skillChecks.failure: Manifest is straightforward — just a shipping record and pickup time.

skillChecks.context: Verifying Switch's decryption is genuine.
skillChecks.success: Decryption appears complete and unaltered.
skillChecks.failure: No way to independently verify.

environmentMechanics.name: Switch's Security Systems
environmentMechanics.trigger: Any aggressive action or theft attempt
environmentMechanics.effect: Hidden ceiling turrets deploy. Motion sensors track all movement. Single exit locks remotely.
environmentMechanics.mitigation: Don't threaten the droid. Treat this as a business meeting.

environmentMechanics.name: Cramped Quarters
environmentMechanics.effect: Narrow bunker cluttered with droid parts. No flanking. Melee range only.
environmentMechanics.mitigation: Use clutter for cover. Japor desk = indestructible hard cover.

intel: Cylinder contains a shipping manifest, not vault coordinates
The Accountant is frozen in carbonite at Niima Landing Field
Imperial pickup is imminent
Manifest references 'Project Leviathan'

pacing.openingBeat: Descent into Switch's bunker beneath the Starship Graveyard. First impression: this droid is more than he seems.
pacing.risingAction: Negotiation. Switch names his price. Maya argues.
pacing.climax: The decryption reveal — the job just changed.
pacing.resolution: Heroes know about The Accountant. Clock is ticking.

scenes.title: The Gangster's Revenge
scenes.readAloud:
The bunker's motion sensors scream. Before you can react, the hatch above explodes inward. Standing in the smoking frame is a massive Chevin — Ganga Lor, Darga the Hutt's lieutenant. His trunk curls with fury as he levels a heavy blaster at the room.

"Nobody steals from the Mighty Darga," he growls, his voice like grinding stone. "Not the girl. Not the droid. And definitely not you."
scenes.gmNotes: Ganga Lor wants to make an example of everyone involved to maintain Darga's reputation. The fight takes place in cramped quarters — use the environment: toppling droid part stacks, shooting steam pipes, activating Switch's security turrets. After defeating Ganga Lor, Switch finishes the analysis and reveals The Accountant: an Imperial logistics officer frozen in carbonite, held at the Niima Landing Field, waiting for Imperial pickup.

npcs.name: Ganga Lor
npcs.notes: Darga's enforcer. Massive trunked alien. Making an example.
npcs.behavior: Leads from front. Uses size to dominate enclosed space. Targets Maya. Fights to the death rather than face Darga.
dialogue: "Nobody steals from the Mighty Darga. Not the girl. Not the droid. And definitely not you."
"Bring me the cylinder and the girl. The rest of you can walk."
"Darga's patience has a limit. Mine ended ten minutes ago."
npcs.intel: Knows exactly what was stolen — the Code Cylinder. Does NOT know what's on it.

npcs.name: Enforcers
npcs.notes: Mixed species — two humans, a Trandoshan, a Nikto.
npcs.behavior: Stack behind Ganga Lor. Enter in pairs. Spread to cover angles. Experienced street fighters.

scenes.hazards: Cramped bunker — collapsing droid stacks, steam pipes, Switch's security turrets can be activated as environmental weapons.

decisions.consequence: Brutal close-quarters combat. Environmental hazards are key.

decisions.choice: Use Switch's turrets against the attackers
decisions.consequence: Switch may help if his life is threatened — but he'll charge for it later.

encounters.name: Ganga Lor's Raid
encounters.trigger: Motion sensors scream. Hatch explodes inward.
encounters.description: Ganga Lor and four enforcers breach Switch's bunker. Close-quarters combat with environmental hazards. Switch's turrets can be turned against attackers.
encounters.tactics: Ganga Lor bulldozes through cover. Enforcers enter in pairs, flanking. They target Maya first.

composition.terrain: Switch's bunker — narrow corridors, droid stacks, steam pipes
composition.positioning: Ganga Lor breaches from above, enforcers enter in pairs behind

skillChecks.context: Activating Switch's ceiling turrets against intruders.
skillChecks.success: Turrets deploy and fire on Ganga Lor's crew. Suppressive fire.
skillChecks.failure: Turrets activate but target indiscriminately.

skillChecks.context: Toppling droid stacks onto enforcers.
skillChecks.success: Cascade buries two enforcers, pinning them.
skillChecks.failure: Stack shifts but doesn't fall convincingly.

skillChecks.context: Shooting out steam pipes for a scalding barrier.
skillChecks.success: Steam fills corridor, forcing enforcers back. Creates concealment.
skillChecks.failure: Pipe bursts unpredictably — could hit allies.

skillChecks.context: Getting Maya behind cover when enforcers target her.
skillChecks.success: Maya is shielded and stays down.
skillChecks.failure: Maya takes a hit — serious medical need.

environmentMechanics.name: Collapsing Droid Stacks
environmentMechanics.effect: Scrap cascades down — difficult terrain, pins combatants.
environmentMechanics.mitigation: Stay clear of stacks or use them as area denial.

environmentMechanics.name: Steam Pipes
environmentMechanics.effect: Superheated steam — burn damage in a line, concealment.
environmentMechanics.mitigation: Breath mask reduces damage. Stay low — steam rises.

environmentMechanics.name: Switch's Security Turrets
environmentMechanics.effect: Ceiling turrets fire on hostiles. Suppressive fire.
environmentMechanics.mitigation: Switch controls targeting. Specify targets or turrets fire on everyone.

intel: Cylinder was in Darga's secure warehouse
Darga has a bounty on Maya
Switch reveals The Accountant's location post-fight

pacing.openingBeat: Motion sensors scream. Explosion from above. Ganga Lor fills the breach.
pacing.risingAction: Close-quarters combat. Environment is the key — turrets, steam, scrap.
pacing.climax: Ganga Lor pushes through to Maya. Someone has to stop him.
pacing.resolution: Ganga Lor falls. Switch reveals The Accountant — carbonite at Niima Landing Field.

scenes.title: The Heist
scenes.readAloud:
The Niima Landing Field is a militarized zone on the outskirts of the outpost. Through your macrobinoculars, you see the hangar — and your first true sign of Imperial occupation. A squad of Stormtroopers in transition-era armor, dirty from the desert, oversees the transfer of cargo. Among the crates sits a carbonite block, frost still clinging to its surface.

An Imperial shuttle is on final approach. The clock is ticking.
scenes.gmNotes: This is the heroes' first brush with the Young Empire. The warlord's thugs guard the perimeter; inside are real Stormtroopers. Options: bluff as the Imperial pickup crew, sneak through maintenance vents, or direct assault using heavy machinery as cover. The carbonite block contains The Accountant. Once secured, the Imperial shuttle lands and the heroes must flee under fire — high-octane exit from Jakku.

npcs.name: Stormtrooper Squad
npcs.notes: Transition-era armor, dirty from desert. Overseeing transfer.
npcs.behavior: Professional but bored. Two on carbonite, two on perimeter, two at shuttle pad. Follow procedure.
dialogue: "Cargo manifest shows one carbonite unit. Confirm for transfer."
"Halt! Present your authorization codes."
"All units, unauthorized personnel in the hangar. Lock it down."
npcs.intel: Real Imperial soldiers. Operate with discipline. Call for backup. Killing troopers has consequences.

npcs.name: Warlord's Guards
npcs.notes: Perimeter security. Lazy and bribeable.
npcs.behavior: Complacent. More interested in next meal than dying for a cargo transfer. Can be bribed (200 credits).
dialogue: "I don't care what's in the box. I just get paid to stand here."
"You got a work order? No? Then walk away."
npcs.intel: Know the block arrived three days ago. Know it's 'some Imperial prisoner' but nothing more.

scenes.hazards: Militarized hangar — heavy machinery, incoming Imperial shuttle providing time pressure. Open terrain with limited cover.

decisions.consequence: High reward if successful — walk right in. High risk of exposure.
Slower but safer approach. May get caught in tight spaces.
Loud and fast. Heavy machinery provides cover but attracts reinforcements.

narrativeLinks.note: The Accountant's reveal about Varth's prison sets up the Ajan Kloss extraction.

encounters.name: Hangar Approach
encounters.trigger: Heroes must cross open ground to the hangar
encounters.description: Three approaches: bluff as Imperial pickup crew, sneak through maintenance vents, or direct assault with heavy machinery cover.
encounters.tactics: Perimeter guards are outer layer (lazy). Stormtroopers are inner layer (disciplined). Getting past guards is easy; troopers are the real challenge.

encounters.name: Carbonite Extraction
encounters.trigger: Heroes reach the carbonite block
encounters.description: Block is heavy (two people or repulsor cart) and connected to monitoring station. Disconnecting triggers 60-second alarm delay.
encounters.tactics: Speed is everything. Imperial shuttle visible on approach.

encounters.name: The Hot Exit
encounters.trigger: Alarm sounds or shuttle lands
encounters.description: Running firefight across landing field. Shuttle disgorges reinforcements. Must reach ship with carbonite block.
encounters.tactics: Stormtroopers establish firing lines. Heavy machinery provides mobile cover but explosive hazards.

composition.terrain: Open landing field with heavy machinery
composition.positioning: Stormtroopers in firing lines, reinforcements from shuttle

skillChecks.context: Impersonating Imperial pickup crew to walk in.
skillChecks.success: Troopers accept credentials. Walk in unchallenged.
skillChecks.failure: Codes don't match. Alarm raised.

skillChecks.context: Crawling through maintenance vents.
skillChecks.success: Emerge inside hangar behind trooper positions.
skillChecks.failure: Vent grate clangs. Nearest trooper investigates.

skillChecks.context: Disconnecting carbonite monitoring without alarm.
skillChecks.success: Clean disconnect. Head start.
skillChecks.failure: Alarm triggers immediately.

skillChecks.context: Flying out under fire with carbonite strapped to hull.
skillChecks.success: Clean escape. Break atmosphere before fighters scramble.
skillChecks.failure: Ship takes hits. Engine/hull damage.

environmentMechanics.name: Open Terrain
environmentMechanics.effect: Landing field is flat and exposed. No natural cover.
environmentMechanics.mitigation: Use heavy machinery as mobile cover.

environmentMechanics.name: Imperial Shuttle Countdown
environmentMechanics.effect: Shuttle on final approach. Visible clock — when it lands, reinforcements pour out.
environmentMechanics.mitigation: Move fast. Every wasted round brings shuttle closer.

environmentMechanics.name: Explosive Fuel Stores
environmentMechanics.trigger: Stray or deliberate shot hits fuel containers
environmentMechanics.effect: Massive explosion — area damage and fire.
environmentMechanics.mitigation: Stay away or use as deliberate diversion.

intel: Empire actively operating on Jakku
Carbonite destined for Imperial facility
Stormtrooper presence confirms Imperial interest in The Accountant

pacing.openingBeat: Recon through macrobinoculars. Hangar, troopers, carbonite, shuttle on approach.
pacing.risingAction: The approach — whichever method chosen. Tension builds near inner perimeter.
pacing.climax: Extraction. Alarms. Blaster fire. Shuttle landing.
pacing.resolution: Heroes escape Jakku with carbonite. First act ends in hyperspace.

scenes.title: The Banshee
scenes.readAloud:
Safe in hyperspace, you thaw the carbonite block. The Accountant coughs to life, disoriented and terrified. When you demand the location of the credits, he laughs bitterly.

"Varth is the only one with the codes. And Varth isn't dead or hiding in luxury. He's being held at a secret prison on the jungle moon of Ajan Kloss. The warlord holding him is about to trade him to the Empire. If you want your payday, you have to break him out — before the Empire takes custody."
scenes.gmNotes: The heroes' contact (Maya or Switch) directs them to Captain Sirona Okeefe and the Banshee. Okeefe is a veteran smuggler who offers a proprietary smuggler's vector through the Imperial blockade in exchange for a cut. The heroes must pilot following her telemetry through debris fields. Upon arriving at Ajan Kloss, they detect a Victory-class Star Destroyer and V-wing patrols.

npcs.name: The Accountant
npcs.notes: Just thawed from carbonite. Terrified but has critical intel.
npcs.behavior: Disoriented and frightened. Cooperative once he realizes the heroes aren't Imperial. Becomes increasingly agitated as he reveals Varth's situation — he knows the timeline is tight.
dialogue: "Varth is the only one with the codes. And Varth isn't dead or hiding in luxury."
"He's being held at a secret prison on the jungle moon of Ajan Kloss. The warlord is about to trade him to the Empire."
"If you want your payday, you have to break him out — before the Empire takes custody."
npcs.intel: The Accountant was Varth's logistics officer. He was frozen because he knew the shipping routes for Project Leviathan. He knows Varth embezzled a fortune but doesn't have the codes himself.

npcs.name: Captain Sirona Okeefe
npcs.notes: Pilots the Banshee, a modified Baudo-class star yacht. Professional and competent.
npcs.behavior: All business. Respects competence, despises whiners. Will risk her ship for the right price but not for free. She's done this run before — blockade experience shows.
dialogue: "I know a vector through the Rishi Maze debris field. Uncharted. Dangerous. But it avoids the Imperial patrol lanes."
"My ship, my rules. You shoot from my turrets, you follow my calls."
"The fee is non-negotiable. I'm not running a charity for broke spacers."
npcs.intel: Okeefe has smuggled through this sector for years. She knows the Victory-class Star Destroyer 'Assiduous' patrols the Ajan Kloss approach. She's heard rumors about Skarn's fortress.

scenes.hazards: Imperial blockade — Venator-class Star Destroyers patrolling the hyperlanes. Dangerous smuggler's vector through debris fields.

decisions.consequence: She uploads the smuggler's vector. Dangerous but the only viable route.

decisions.choice: Try to run the blockade alone
decisions.consequence: Extremely dangerous without the vector data. Near-suicidal.

narrativeLinks.note: Okeefe's loyalty is tested again over Bespin — Varth attempts to sacrifice her.

encounters.name: The Thawing
encounters.trigger: Safe in hyperspace after Jakku escape
encounters.description: The heroes thaw the carbonite block. The Accountant revives — disoriented, terrified, and immediately delivering bad news. The job has changed again.
encounters.tactics: The Accountant is cooperative but desperate. He knows the timeline is tight and tries to convey urgency.

encounters.name: The Blockade Run
encounters.trigger: Approaching Ajan Kloss system
encounters.description: Okeefe's smuggler's vector through the Rishi Maze debris field. The heroes must assist with navigation, turret defense, and damage control as they thread past Imperial patrols.
encounters.tactics: The debris field provides cover but also hazards. Imperial V-wing patrols sweep in predictable patterns — timing is crucial.

skillChecks.context: Treating The Accountant's hibernation sickness after carbonite thaw.
skillChecks.success: Sickness fades quickly. The Accountant is lucid and able to provide detailed intel.
skillChecks.failure: Hibernation sickness lingers. Accountant is nauseous and disoriented — information comes in fragments.

skillChecks.context: Assessing whether The Accountant is telling the truth about Varth and the codes.
skillChecks.success: He's telling the truth — he's too scared to lie. But he's also holding something back about Varth's character.
skillChecks.failure: Seems genuine. No red flags detected.

skillChecks.context: Following Okeefe's telemetry through the debris field while avoiding patrols.
skillChecks.success: Clean run through the debris. Arrive at Ajan Kloss undetected.
skillChecks.failure: Clipped by debris or detected by a patrol. The ship takes damage and the approach is compromised.

skillChecks.context: Repairing damage sustained during the blockade run.
skillChecks.success: Systems patched. Ship is operational for atmospheric entry.
skillChecks.failure: Repairs are temporary. Ship is fragile going into atmosphere.

environmentMechanics.name: Rishi Maze Debris Field
environmentMechanics.effect: Massive chunks of ancient wreckage. Provides concealment from sensors but requires constant course corrections. Collision risk on failed piloting checks.
environmentMechanics.mitigation: Follow Okeefe's telemetry precisely. Co-pilot assistance steps up the check.

environmentMechanics.name: Imperial Patrol Patterns
environmentMechanics.effect: V-wing patrols sweep the approaches on predictable schedules. Being caught in the open triggers pursuit.
environmentMechanics.mitigation: Time the approach between patrol sweeps. Investigation check reveals the pattern.

environmentMechanics.name: Carbonite Hibernation Sickness
environmentMechanics.effect: Temporary blindness, disorientation, nausea. The Accountant can't function until treated.
environmentMechanics.mitigation: Medicine check to accelerate recovery. Without treatment, lasts 1-2 hours.

intel: Varth is held at 'The Vanishing Place' on Ajan Kloss
General Skarn controls the prison — a warlord, not Imperial military
Inquisitor Draco is expected to take custody imminently
Varth has the codes to an embezzled fortune
Project Leviathan is referenced but details remain unclear

pacing.openingBeat: Hyperspace. Safety — for now. The carbonite block sits in the hold, frost still clinging.
pacing.risingAction: The thawing. The Accountant's reveal. The job changes for the third time — from heist to prison break.
pacing.climax: The blockade run. Threading through debris and patrols.
pacing.resolution: Arrival at Ajan Kloss. The jungle moon fills the viewport. A Star Destroyer hangs in orbit.

scenes.title: The Crash
scenes.readAloud:
You break the cloud layer and the lush, overgrown jungle of Ajan Kloss rushes up to meet you. The moon is a Green Hell — massive ancient trees, fungal overgrowth, and air so thick with humidity your viewport fogs instantly.

A glancing hit from a perimeter defense turret clips your wing. The ship shears through the treeline, branches snapping like bones, and skids to a halt in the mud. You're safe from sensors — but grounded, miles from the fortress.
scenes.gmNotes: The ship is damaged and grounded until repairs are made. The heroes must proceed on foot through hostile jungle to reach Skarn's fortress, 'The Vanishing Place.' This establishes the environmental threat of Ajan Kloss and forces the heroes into the wilderness.

npcs.name: Captain Sirona Okeefe
npcs.notes: At the helm during the crash. Keeps her composure under fire.
npcs.behavior: Okeefe is calm under pressure. She manages the crash landing with professional detachment, calling out altitude and obstacle warnings. After the crash, she immediately assesses her ship and becomes protective of the Banshee — she expects the heroes to help with repairs or at least not make things worse.
dialogue: "Brace for impact — I'm putting us into the canopy. It's going to be rough."
"My ship. My beautiful ship. You're going to help me fix this."
"The fortress is northwest. Twelve klicks through that mess. We better move before a patrol spots the smoke."
npcs.intel: Okeefe knows this jungle from previous smuggling runs to Ajan Kloss. She can identify the most dangerous fauna and knows that the fortress is approximately 12 klicks northwest.

npcs.name: The Accountant
npcs.notes: Still suffering from hibernation sickness. A liability during the trek.
npcs.behavior: The Accountant is weak and disoriented. He slows the group down. He complains but provides useful intel about the fortress when lucid. He's terrified of being recaptured.
dialogue: "I can't... my legs aren't working right. The carbonite..."
"Skarn's fortress has a perimeter defense grid. That's what hit us."
"If the Empire gets me back, I disappear. You understand? I disappear."
npcs.intel: The Accountant recognizes the perimeter turret pattern — he helped design Skarn's defense procurement. He knows the grid has a blind spot on the northeast face where the drainage sluice exits.

scenes.hazards: Crash landing — ship damage, potential injuries. Dense jungle terrain ahead. Perimeter defense turrets.

decisions.consequence: Takes time but ensures an escape route. Risk of Imperial patrols finding the crash site.
Faster but no guaranteed extraction. Ship repairs happen later.

encounters.name: Crash Landing
encounters.trigger: Perimeter turret clips the ship during atmospheric entry
encounters.description: The ship shears through treeline and skids into mud. Heroes must survive the landing and assess damage. Potential injuries from impact.
encounters.tactics: This is environmental — no opponents. The jungle itself is the threat. Quick decisions about priorities: repair vs. move.

encounters.name: Crash Triage
encounters.trigger: Immediately after the crash
encounters.description: Assess injuries, salvage essential gear, determine ship damage severity. Decision point: repair first or push toward the fortress?
encounters.tactics: The longer the heroes stay at the crash site, the higher the risk of Imperial patrols finding the wreckage from the air.

skillChecks.context: Controlled crash landing through dense jungle canopy.
skillChecks.success: Rough but controlled. Ship is damaged but structurally intact. No injuries.
skillChecks.failure: Violent impact. Ship takes severe damage. One or more heroes take Physique trauma from the crash.

skillChecks.context: Assessing the ship's damage after the crash.
skillChecks.success: Clear picture: engines need parts, hull breaches need patching. Estimated repair time: 4-6 hours with proper tools.
skillChecks.failure: Damage is worse than it looks. Hidden structural failures will manifest later.

skillChecks.context: Orienting in the dense jungle. Determining direction to the fortress.
skillChecks.success: Natural navigation using canopy gaps, water flow, and moss patterns. Clear heading established.
skillChecks.failure: The jungle is disorienting. Progress is slow and uncertain.

skillChecks.context: Treating crash injuries — impact trauma, lacerations from debris.
skillChecks.success: Injuries stabilized. Everyone is mobile.
skillChecks.failure: Injuries are field-dressed but not properly treated. Penalties to physical actions until proper care.

environmentMechanics.name: Dense Jungle Canopy
environmentMechanics.effect: Blocks line of sight to sky. Provides concealment from aerial patrols but also blocks navigation reference points.
environmentMechanics.mitigation: Survival check to navigate by natural signs.

environmentMechanics.name: Humid Atmosphere
environmentMechanics.effect: Air is thick with moisture and fungal spores. Breath masks recommended. Without respiratory protection, characters make Endure checks every few hours or suffer fatigue.
environmentMechanics.mitigation: Breath mask eliminates the hazard entirely.

environmentMechanics.name: Crash Site Detection Risk
environmentMechanics.trigger: Each hour spent at the crash site
environmentMechanics.effect: Imperial V-wing patrols may spot the wreckage. Chance increases over time.
environmentMechanics.mitigation: Camouflage the ship (Stealth or Survival check) or move out quickly.

intel: The fortress is approximately 12 klicks northwest through dense jungle
Perimeter defense turrets confirm an active military presence
The jungle itself is a significant threat — acid pools, toxic flora, hostile fauna

pacing.openingBeat: Breaking atmosphere. Lush jungle rushing up. The turret hit. The crash.
pacing.risingAction: Aftermath. Damage assessment. The decision: repair or push forward?
pacing.climax: The realization of how far they are from the fortress and what's between them and it.
pacing.resolution: The heroes set out into the jungle. The Green Hell begins.

scenes.title: The Jungle Trek
scenes.readAloud:
The jungle is alive — and hungry. Every step forward is a negotiation with the terrain. Acid pools disguised as standing water hiss when a thrown rock disturbs them. Fungal blooms the size of speeder bikes pulse with a sickly bioluminescence, and you quickly learn that disturbing them triggers a violent detonation of toxic spores.

Then the ambush comes — not from Imperials, but from a ghost. A lone figure in tattered Separatist commando gear drops from the canopy, rifle aimed at your heads.
scenes.gmNotes: Environmental hazards: Acid Pools (disguised as standing water) and Exploding Fungus Blooms (detonate when disturbed). The ambusher is Vazus Mandrake, a Separatist Holdout Commando who refused the shutdown order 3 years ago. He's been waging guerilla war against Skarn. Once convinced the heroes are against the Empire, he agrees to guide them to a secret drainage sluice into the fortress — in exchange for sabotaging the facility's generator.

npcs.name: Vazus Mandrake
npcs.notes: Weary veteran. Refused the CIS shutdown order. Waging guerilla war against Skarn alone for 3 years.
npcs.behavior: Paranoid and precise. Tests the heroes before revealing himself. Ambushes from the canopy — drops down with rifle aimed. Once convinced they're anti-Empire, becomes a cautious ally.
dialogue: "Don't move. I've had a bead on you since the river crossing."
"You're going to The Vanishing Place? Then we have a common interest. Skarn took something from me too."
"I know a way in — a drainage sluice on the northeast face. But I need something in return. The generator. I need it destroyed."
npcs.intel: Mandrake knows the fortress layout intimately. He's been running recon for years. He knows guard rotations, blind spots, and the drainage sluice entrance. He also knows Vischera is conducting experiments in the Medical Bay.

scenes.hazards: Acid Pools disguised as standing water. Exploding Fungus Blooms that detonate when disturbed. Semi-sentient hostile flora. Lethal fauna.

decisions.consequence: He guides you to a secret entrance. You must sabotage the generator in return.

decisions.choice: Refuse and find your own way in
decisions.consequence: Much harder infiltration without a guide. The fortress is heavily fortified.

encounters.name: Acid Pool Hazard
encounters.trigger: First encountered 2 klicks from the crash site
encounters.description: Standing water that hisses when disturbed. Disguised acid pools — indistinguishable from regular water without testing. A thrown rock triggers the telltale hiss.
encounters.tactics: Environmental hazard. No opponents. Tox detector identifies them automatically. Without one, Survival or Investigation check required.

encounters.name: Exploding Fungus Blooms
encounters.trigger: Encountered along the jungle trail
encounters.description: Massive fungal formations pulse with sickly bioluminescence. Physical contact triggers violent detonation of toxic spores in a 5-meter radius.
encounters.tactics: Avoidance is key. Ranged detonation from safe distance clears the path. Breath mask provides protection against spore damage.

encounters.name: Mandrake's Ambush
encounters.trigger: After navigating through the worst of the jungle hazards
encounters.description: A figure in tattered Separatist commando gear drops from the canopy with a rifle aimed at the heroes. This is first contact with Vazus Mandrake.
encounters.tactics: Mandrake is testing them. He won't shoot unless they shoot first. He's been watching them navigate the jungle — their competence has already told him a lot about who they are.

encounters.name: Mandrake's Deal
encounters.trigger: After Mandrake is convinced the heroes aren't Imperial
encounters.description: Mandrake offers to guide the heroes to a secret entrance in exchange for sabotaging the fortress generator.
encounters.tactics: Mandrake is firm but fair. He won't guide them without the deal. He's been planning this for years and needs the generator down to execute his own endgame.

skillChecks.context: Identifying acid pools before someone steps in one.
skillChecks.success: The subtle differences become clear — slight discoloration, absence of insects, faint chemical smell.
skillChecks.failure: Someone steps in. Acid damage to boots/legs. The pool is shallow but the acid is strong.

skillChecks.context: Navigating difficult jungle terrain — root systems, steep ravines, mudslides.
skillChecks.success: The party makes good time. No injuries from the terrain.
skillChecks.failure: Someone slips or falls. Minor injury, time lost, noise made.

skillChecks.context: Moving through the jungle without alerting patrols or wildlife.
skillChecks.success: Silent progress. No encounters with hostile fauna or Imperial scouts.
skillChecks.failure: Something notices them — a territorial predator, a sensor drone, or worse.

skillChecks.context: Reading Mandrake during his ambush — is he going to shoot?
skillChecks.success: He's testing you. His finger isn't on the trigger. He wants allies, not corpses.
skillChecks.failure: Impossible to read. The tattered uniform and hard eyes give nothing away.

skillChecks.context: Negotiating with Mandrake for guidance without committing to the generator sabotage.
skillChecks.success: Mandrake agrees to guide them to the entrance. The sabotage becomes optional — but he'll be disappointed.
skillChecks.failure: No deal without the sabotage. Mandrake is immovable on this point.

environmentMechanics.name: Acid Pools
environmentMechanics.effect: Disguised as standing water. Contact causes immediate acid damage. Deeper pools can dissolve equipment.
environmentMechanics.mitigation: Tox detector identifies automatically. Thrown rock test. Survival check to spot visual tells.

environmentMechanics.name: Exploding Fungus Blooms
environmentMechanics.trigger: Physical contact with the fungal formations
environmentMechanics.effect: Violent spore detonation in 5m radius. Toxic spores cause Endure trauma and temporary respiratory distress.
environmentMechanics.mitigation: Breath mask blocks spore damage. Ranged detonation from safe distance. Stealth to navigate around.

environmentMechanics.name: Toxic Atmosphere (Canopy Level)
environmentMechanics.effect: Fungal spore density increases near the canopy. Extended exposure without protection causes escalating fatigue.
environmentMechanics.mitigation: Breath mask. Stay low. Move through open clearings when possible.

environmentMechanics.name: Hostile Fauna
environmentMechanics.effect: Ajan Kloss wildlife is territorial and predatory. Large arthropods, hunting birds, venomous ground-crawlers.
environmentMechanics.mitigation: Stealth to avoid. Beast Handling to redirect. Combat as last resort — gunfire attracts attention.

intel: Detailed fortress layout from Mandrake
Guard rotation patterns
Location of the drainage sluice entrance
Captain Vischera's experiments in the Medical Bay
Generator location and vulnerabilities
connections: Vazus Mandrake (guide, conditional ally — depends on sabotage deal)

pacing.openingBeat: The jungle closes in. Every step is a negotiation with the terrain.
pacing.risingAction: Environmental hazards escalate. Acid pools, fungus, hostile fauna.
pacing.climax: Mandrake's ambush. Rifle in your face from the canopy.
pacing.resolution: The deal is struck. Mandrake leads them toward the fortress.

scenes.title: The Vanishing Place — Infiltration
scenes.readAloud:
From the ridge, you see the fortress. It is not a pristine Imperial garrison but a repurposed, moss-covered temple ruin fortified with scavenged Clone Wars technology. Laser fences line the perimeter. Gun emplacements squat on the walls like armored toads.

And there, on the landing pad — a Lambda-class shuttle. A squad of pristine Stormtroopers stands at attention, a stark contrast to Skarn's grimy mercenaries. The Empire is already here.
scenes.gmNotes: Mandrake leads the heroes through the drainage sluice into the fortress interior. Key locations: the Medical Bay (Captain Vischera conducts biological experiments on wildlife) and the Communications Center. Intercepted transmissions reveal that the Imperial officer outside is stalling — Inquisitor Valin Draco is en route to take personal custody of Varth. This creates a hard clock: grab Varth before the Inquisitor lands.

npcs.name: Captain Vischera
npcs.notes: On loan to Skarn. Conducting biological experiments in the Medical Bay.
npcs.behavior: Cold, clinical, and fascinated by the jungle's biology. Not a fighter. Will surrender if cornered. Tries to negotiate with scientific data as leverage.
dialogue: "I'm a researcher, not a soldier. The specimens here are worth more than whatever you're after."
"You want to know what Skarn is really doing here? I can tell you. Just don't touch my samples."
"The Imperial officer in the holding cells? Charming man. Completely amoral."
npcs.intel: Vischera knows Draco is coming specifically for Varth. She knows Skarn is stalling the Imperial handoff to extract more payment. She's seen Varth's data cylinders and knows they contain financial records.

npcs.name: Skarn's Mercenaries
npcs.notes: Grimy, undisciplined compared to Stormtroopers. Mixed species.
npcs.behavior: Patrol in pairs. Bored and complacent in the fortress interior. Alert near the detention block and communications center. Respond to alarms slowly.
dialogue: "Did you hear something in the sluice?"
"Skarn says no one goes near the Imperial's cell without authorization."
npcs.intel: The mercs know Skarn is playing a dangerous game with the Empire. Morale is low — they're afraid of Draco.

npcs.name: Stormtrooper Squad
npcs.notes: Pristine armor. At the landing pad near the shuttle. Separate from Skarn's people.
npcs.behavior: Maintain their own perimeter. Don't mix with mercs. Professional and alert. Answer only to the Imperial officer on site.
dialogue: "This area is under Imperial jurisdiction. Move along."
npcs.intel: The troopers know Draco is en route. They're the advance team. Their orders are to secure the landing pad and wait.

scenes.hazards: Fortified temple ruin — laser fences, gun emplacements, Clone Wars-era tech security. Hard clock: Inquisitor Draco is en route.

decisions.choice: Sabotage the generator per Mandrake's deal
decisions.consequence: Creates chaos and a distraction. Alerts the garrison but disables many defenses.

decisions.choice: Skip the sabotage and head straight for Varth
decisions.consequence: Faster but Mandrake may refuse further help. Full security remains active.

encounters.name: The Drainage Sluice
encounters.trigger: Mandrake leads them to the northeast face
encounters.description: A drainage tunnel barely wide enough for a Wookiee. Knee-deep in runoff. Leads into the fortress basement level. Trip wires and crude motion sensors line the route.
encounters.tactics: Slow, careful progress. Skulduggery to disarm traps. Tech to bypass sensors. Athletics for the tight squeeze.

encounters.name: The Medical Bay
encounters.trigger: Optional — the heroes can explore the medical bay
encounters.description: Vischera's laboratory. Specimens in tanks. Data terminals with research logs. Vischera herself is here, working late.
encounters.tactics: Vischera is not hostile. She can be interrogated, intimidated, or bargained with. She values her research above her loyalty to Skarn.

encounters.name: The Communications Center
encounters.trigger: Optional — the heroes can access the comms center
encounters.description: Intercepting transmissions reveals that Inquisitor Valin Draco is en route. This creates the hard clock: get Varth before Draco lands.
encounters.tactics: Tech or Investigation check to decrypt the transmissions. The clock starts NOW.

encounters.name: Generator Sabotage
encounters.trigger: If the heroes honor Mandrake's deal
encounters.description: The fortress generator is a repurposed Clone Wars power unit. Sabotaging it kills lights, disables laser fences, and creates chaos. But it also alerts the entire garrison.
encounters.tactics: Tech check to sabotage cleanly. The explosion is impressive and immediate. Mandrake uses the distraction for his own strike.

skillChecks.context: Disarming trip wires and crude sensors in the drainage sluice.
skillChecks.success: Clean passage. No alarms triggered.
skillChecks.failure: A sensor trips. Distant alarm sounds. Guards begin investigating.

skillChecks.context: Moving through fortress corridors without alerting patrols.
skillChecks.success: Ghost-like progress. Patrols pass without noticing.
skillChecks.failure: A patrol spots movement. Alert is raised in that section.

skillChecks.context: Hacking the communications center to intercept transmissions.
skillChecks.success: Draco's ETA is confirmed. The heroes know exactly how much time they have.
skillChecks.failure: Partial intercept — they know someone important is coming, but not who or when exactly.

skillChecks.context: Sabotaging the generator per Mandrake's deal.
skillChecks.success: Clean sabotage with delayed detonation — heroes can get clear before it blows.
skillChecks.failure: Immediate detonation. Heroes are caught in the blast radius. Chaos starts now whether they're ready or not.

skillChecks.context: Examining Vischera's research and data terminals.
skillChecks.success: Detailed information about Skarn's operation, Varth's status, and references to Project Leviathan.
skillChecks.failure: Research is encrypted beyond easy access. Only surface-level observations.

environmentMechanics.name: Laser Fences
environmentMechanics.trigger: Active along perimeter and key corridors
environmentMechanics.effect: Lethal energy barriers. Contact causes severe damage. Block passage entirely.
environmentMechanics.mitigation: Generator sabotage disables all laser fences. Tech check can disable individual sections.

environmentMechanics.name: Clone Wars Security Systems
environmentMechanics.effect: Aging but functional security cameras, motion sensors, and automated turrets in key areas.
environmentMechanics.mitigation: Tech or Skulduggery to bypass. Generator sabotage knocks them all offline.

environmentMechanics.name: Draco Clock
environmentMechanics.trigger: Activated when heroes intercept communications (or after 45 real-world minutes)
environmentMechanics.effect: Inquisitor Draco is en route. Hard deadline. If the heroes are still inside when he arrives, the difficulty escalates dramatically.
environmentMechanics.mitigation: Move fast. Prioritize Varth over exploration.

intel: Inquisitor Draco is en route — hard clock
Skarn is stalling the Imperial handoff for more money
Varth has data cylinders he considers essential
Vischera's research hints at Project Leviathan's biological component
connections: Vazus Mandrake (solidified if generator is sabotaged)

pacing.openingBeat: The fortress from the ridge. Moss-covered temple ruin. Laser fences. Gun emplacements. The Lambda shuttle on the pad.
pacing.risingAction: The sluice. The corridors. Optional encounters — medbay, comms, generator. Information and choices.
pacing.climax: The Draco clock starts. Time is running out.
pacing.resolution: The heroes reach the detention block level. Varth is close.

scenes.title: The Detention Block
scenes.readAloud:
You blow the door to the holding cell expecting a broken man. Instead, you find Admiral Gilder Varth sitting comfortably, gambling with a confused guard and packing a bag.

He looks up with a charming, tired smile and flips a credit chip in greeting. "You're late. I had credits on the second moonrise."
scenes.gmNotes: Varth is arrogant, charming, and unbothered. He treats the rescue like a hired service. He refuses to leave without his encrypted data cylinders, which he claims contain the ledger for Project Leviathan. Key reveal: he didn't defect for moral reasons — he embezzled a fortune and needs the heroes to get him and his money out. He uses nicknames: 'Kid,' 'Pilot,' 'Heavy.' This is the heroes' first real interaction with Varth — establish his 'Cool Uncle' persona.

npcs.name: Admiral Gilder Varth
npcs.notes: The campaign's central NPC. Charming, cynical, self-serving. Refuses to leave without his data cylinders.
npcs.behavior: Treats the rescue like a hired service. Completely unbothered. Gambling with his guard when the heroes arrive. Uses nicknames: 'Kid,' 'Pilot,' 'Heavy.' Establishes his 'Cool Uncle' persona immediately.
dialogue: "You're late. I had credits on the second moonrise."
"I appreciate the dramatic entrance, but I'm not leaving without my cylinders. They're in Skarn's vault — second floor, east wing."
"Don't look at me like that. Those cylinders are worth more than everything in this fortress combined. Including me."
"Call me Varth. Everyone does. Even people who want me dead — which is most of them."
npcs.intel: Varth embezzled a fortune from Project Leviathan. His data cylinders contain the financial ledger — account numbers, routing codes, and the encryption keys. He didn't defect for moral reasons. He's a grifter in an admiral's uniform.

scenes.hazards: Detention block — automated defenses, elite guards. Time pressure from Inquisitor's arrival.

decisions.consequence: Fastest exit but Varth refuses to cooperate without his cylinders. May need to drag him.
Takes extra time and risk but secures Varth's full cooperation and the Project Leviathan data.

narrativeLinks.note: Varth immediately begins directing the heroes toward Darga the Hutt.

encounters.name: Meeting Varth
encounters.trigger: Heroes breach the detention block
encounters.description: Varth is not a broken prisoner — he's been waiting. He's already packed. The guard is confused and half-charmed. Varth treats this like a business meeting, not a rescue.
encounters.tactics: Varth controls the conversation. He assesses the heroes immediately and decides how to handle each one. He's testing them.

encounters.name: The Cylinder Retrieval
encounters.trigger: Varth insists on retrieving his data cylinders from Skarn's vault
encounters.description: Side mission under time pressure. The vault is on the second floor, east wing. Two guards, an electronic lock, and Skarn's paranoia.
encounters.tactics: Speed vs. thoroughness. The Draco clock is still running. Every minute spent here is a minute less for escape.

skillChecks.context: Reading Varth — is he an ally, a liability, or something worse?
skillChecks.success: Varth is charming because it's a survival strategy. He's genuinely brilliant, genuinely selfish, and genuinely dangerous. He will betray anyone if the math works out.
skillChecks.failure: He seems like a charismatic, slightly eccentric officer who got in over his head.

skillChecks.context: Convincing Varth to leave WITHOUT the cylinders.
skillChecks.success: Varth reluctantly agrees — but makes it clear this will cost them later. He holds it over the heroes.
skillChecks.failure: Varth flatly refuses. 'Without those cylinders, I'm just a dead admiral. With them, I'm rich. We're getting the cylinders.'

skillChecks.context: Building rapport with Varth during the initial meeting.
skillChecks.success: Varth warms to the heroes. Gives them a genuine piece of advice about Draco: 'He doesn't negotiate. He collects.'
skillChecks.failure: Varth is polite but guarded. Treats the heroes as hired help.

skillChecks.context: Cracking Skarn's vault to retrieve the data cylinders.
skillChecks.success: Clean entry. Cylinders secured. No additional alarms.
skillChecks.failure: The vault alarm triggers. Guards converge on the east wing.
skillChecks.isGated: Only if heroes agree to retrieve Varth's cylinders

skillChecks.context: Examining the cylinders while Varth isn't watching.
skillChecks.success: The cylinders contain financial records — massive sums routed through shell accounts. 'Project Leviathan' appears repeatedly.
skillChecks.failure: The cylinders are heavily encrypted. Can't read them without specialized equipment.

environmentMechanics.name: Draco Clock (Continued)
environmentMechanics.effect: The clock keeps ticking. Every detour — including the cylinder retrieval — eats into escape time.
environmentMechanics.mitigation: Move fast. Make hard choices about what's worth the time.

environmentMechanics.name: Detention Block Security
environmentMechanics.effect: Heavier guard presence than other fortress sections. Automated cell locks. Surveillance cameras.
environmentMechanics.mitigation: Generator sabotage (if completed in Scene 8) disables most of this.

environmentMechanics.name: Varth's Cooperation
environmentMechanics.effect: Varth fully cooperates and shares intel only if his cylinders are secured. Without them, he's sulky, uncooperative, and may try to escape on his own later.
environmentMechanics.mitigation: Get the cylinders. Or be very persuasive.

intel: Varth embezzled from Project Leviathan
The codes to his fortune are held by his former partner — Darga the Hutt
Project Leviathan is described as a 'mobile siege platform in the Unknown Regions'
Varth's warning about Draco: 'He doesn't negotiate. He collects.'
connections: Admiral Gilder Varth (the campaign's central NPC — charming, unreliable, essential)

pacing.openingBeat: Blow the door expecting a broken man. Find Varth gambling with his guard.
pacing.risingAction: Varth's demands. The cylinder retrieval. Time pressure mounting.
pacing.climax: The choice: get the cylinders and risk Draco, or drag Varth out now.
pacing.resolution: Varth is secured. The heroes have what they came for — maybe more.

scenes.title: The Deal Gone Bad
scenes.subtitle: Climax
scenes.readAloud:
The landing pad erupts. The Stormtroopers open fire on Skarn's mercenaries. The fortress becomes a three-way warzone — Imperials, the Warlord's forces, and you caught in the middle.

Then a shadow falls over the courtyard. A shuttle screams overhead, engines howling, and you see the crimson markings of the Inquisitorius. Draco has arrived.

Mandrake grabs your arm. "Run. Into the jungle. NOW."
scenes.gmNotes: Three-way firefight: Imperials vs. Warlord vs. Heroes. Draco's shuttle deploys Shadow Troopers to cut off retreat. Captain Okeefe swoops in with the Banshee (or the heroes' repaired ship), strafing Imperial positions to buy time. The heroes blast off as Imperial walkers breach the treeline. In hyperspace, Varth reveals Project Leviathan is a massive mobile siege platform in the Unknown Regions, and the codes to his fortune are held by his former partner — Darga the Hutt. This sets up Adventure 2.

npcs.name: Inquisitor Valin Draco
npcs.notes: Arrives via shuttle. The campaign's recurring villain. Does not appear in person in this scene — glimpsed from distance.
npcs.behavior: Draco does not engage the heroes directly in this scene. He arrives, deploys his Shadow Troopers, and begins methodically securing the fortress. The heroes see him from a distance — a dark figure descending from the shuttle ramp. This is a preview, not a confrontation.
npcs.intel: Draco is an Inquisitor — a Force-sensitive Imperial operative. He is here specifically for Varth. The heroes will face him directly in later adventures.

npcs.name: Shadow Troopers
npcs.notes: Black-armored elite commandos. Stealth suits. Cutting off retreat.
npcs.behavior: Deployed to the jungle perimeter. They move through the trees like ghosts. Their job is to prevent anyone leaving the fortress alive. They are significantly more dangerous than standard Stormtroopers.
npcs.intel: Shadow Troopers are Inquisitorius assets — black ops. Stealth-equipped, enhanced training, independent targeting.

npcs.name: Captain Okeefe
npcs.notes: Provides air support and extraction.
npcs.behavior: Okeefe brings the Banshee in hot, strafing Imperial positions to buy the heroes time. She's risking everything — this is beyond her contract.
dialogue: "I see you! Get to the clearing — I'm making one pass!"
"That's an Inquisitor shuttle. You didn't mention an Inquisitor. My fee just tripled."

scenes.hazards: Three-way firefight. Imperial walkers approaching. Shadow Troopers flanking. Time-critical extraction.

decisions.choice: Fight through to the landing pad
decisions.consequence: Direct but dangerous — heavy Imperial presence.

decisions.choice: Flee into the jungle for Okeefe's pickup
decisions.consequence: Must evade Shadow Troopers in dense canopy. Okeefe provides strafing support.

narrativeLinks.note: Varth's reveal about Darga holding the encryption keys launches Adventure 2.

encounters.name: Three-Way Firefight
encounters.trigger: The landing pad erupts as Imperials and Skarn's forces clash
encounters.description: The fortress becomes a warzone. Three factions: Imperials securing the perimeter, Skarn's mercenaries defending their territory, and the heroes trying to escape through the middle.
encounters.tactics: Use the chaos. The Imperials and mercs are fighting each other — the heroes can exploit the confusion to slip through. But Shadow Troopers are specifically hunting for anyone trying to leave.

composition.positioning: Imperials and mercs fighting each other; heroes threading through

encounters.name: Shadow Trooper Ambush
encounters.trigger: When the heroes reach the jungle perimeter
encounters.description: Four Shadow Troopers emerge from concealment in the treeline. Black armor, stealth suits, precision fire. They are the real obstacle to escape.
encounters.tactics: Shadow Troopers fight smart — overlapping fields of fire, tactical movement, targeting the weakest member. They don't charge; they control the engagement distance.

composition.positioning: Concealed in canopy, overlapping fields of fire

encounters.name: Okeefe's Extraction
encounters.trigger: After clearing or bypassing the Shadow Troopers
encounters.description: Okeefe swoops in with the Banshee, strafing Imperial positions. The heroes must reach the clearing and board under fire. Varth provides coordinates for the hyperspace jump.
encounters.tactics: Running to the ship while Okeefe covers them. Someone needs to man the dorsal turret. The ship takes hits during liftoff.

skillChecks.context: Reading the three-way battle to find the safest path through.
skillChecks.success: A gap in the fighting — the heroes can move through with minimal contact.
skillChecks.failure: No clear path. They have to fight through at least one engagement.

skillChecks.context: Dodging through crossfire between Imperial and mercenary positions.
skillChecks.success: Clean movement through the kill zone. No hits taken.
skillChecks.failure: Caught in crossfire. Ranged damage from stray shots.

skillChecks.context: Engaging the Shadow Troopers in the treeline.
skillChecks.success: Precise fire drops or suppresses them. Path to the clearing opens.
skillChecks.failure: Shadow Troopers return devastating fire from cover. Heroes are pinned.

skillChecks.context: Sprint to Okeefe's ship across open ground.
skillChecks.success: Full speed. Board the ship. Go.
skillChecks.failure: Stumble, exhaustion, or carrying wounded slows the run. More time under fire.

skillChecks.context: Liftoff under fire. Breaking atmosphere with a Star Destroyer in orbit.
skillChecks.success: Clean escape. Jump to hyperspace before fighters can intercept.
skillChecks.failure: Ship takes heavy damage during liftoff. Emergency repairs needed in hyperspace.

environmentMechanics.name: Three-Way Crossfire
environmentMechanics.effect: Stray fire from all directions. Moving through contested areas without cover risks catching bolts from any faction.
environmentMechanics.mitigation: Use Tactics to read the battle. Move during lulls. Use smoke grenades for concealment.

environmentMechanics.name: Shadow Trooper Stealth
environmentMechanics.effect: Shadow Troopers are nearly invisible in the treeline. First shot comes without warning.
environmentMechanics.mitigation: Togruta echolocation, Rodian infrared, or Cathar hearing can detect them before visual contact. Investigation check to spot thermal shimmer.

environmentMechanics.name: Imperial Walker Approach
environmentMechanics.trigger: After 3-4 combat rounds in the fortress
environmentMechanics.effect: AT-DP walkers breach the treeline. Heavy weapons fire. The fortress is about to be overrun.
environmentMechanics.mitigation: Leave NOW. The walkers make escape on foot nearly impossible.

environmentMechanics.name: Okeefe's Strafing Window
environmentMechanics.effect: Okeefe provides one strafing pass that suppresses Imperial positions for 2 rounds. After that, she has to pull up or get shot down.
environmentMechanics.mitigation: Time the run to the clearing with Okeefe's pass.

intel: Project Leviathan is a massive mobile siege platform being built in the Unknown Regions
The encryption keys to Varth's fortune are held by Darga the Hutt — his former partner
Inquisitor Draco is now aware the heroes exist
Varth reveals the setup for Adventure 2: they must go to Darga to get the keys
connections: Captain Okeefe (loyalty proven — she risked everything)
Admiral Varth (the 'Cool Uncle' — charming, dangerous, essential)

pacing.openingBeat: The landing pad erupts. Stormtroopers vs. mercenaries. Draco's shuttle screams overhead.
pacing.risingAction: Running through a three-way firefight. Shadow Troopers in the treeline. Walkers approaching.
pacing.climax: The Shadow Trooper ambush at the perimeter. The hardest fight of the adventure.
pacing.resolution: Okeefe's extraction. Liftoff under fire. Hyperspace. Varth's reveal: Darga the Hutt holds the keys. Adventure 2 begins.

adventures.title: A Wretched Hive
adventures.summary: The heroes travel to Takodana to infiltrate Darga the Hutt's fortress, posing as entertainment to steal the encryption keys to Varth's fortune. They discover a captive Jedi Master and must choose between the mission and their conscience.

gearNotes.reason: Varth coaches the heroes over encrypted comms from outside Darga's palace. Open comlinks in a Hutt fortress means blown cover. Hard permission gate for the entire Long Con sequence.
The entire mission objective is slicing Darga's datapad to extract the encryption keys. Without a proper slicing interface, the core technical challenge is stepped down or impossible.
High-security detention block locks where Jedi Master Denia is held. Without one, bypassing these suffers heavy penalties.
The Tech's audition — ordered to fix Darga's hover-sled with a blaster at their head. No toolkit means stepped-down rolls on a pass/fail moment.
